input.prompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm coming from ASP.NET Core and I registered my DbContext with
AddScoped in my MAUI app. My data seems to persist across page
navigations when it shouldn't. What's going on?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common pitfall when moving from ASP.NET Core to .NET MAUI regarding dependency injection scoping — specifically why `AddScoped` behaves differently in MAUI than in ASP.NET Core.

This is a conceptual question I can answer directly without needing to explore any codebase.

In ASP.NET Core, a "scope" is created per HTTP request, so `AddScoped` gives you a new instance per request. In MAUI, there's no concept of an HTTP request — the app has a single scope for its entire lifetime (the app-level `IServiceProvider`). So `AddScoped` effectively behaves like `AddSingleton` in MAUI, meaning your DbContext is reused across all page navigations.
